steps.title: Identify Trever's decisive counterpoint
steps.content: Trever directly contests the “hyper-realistic” interpretation, arguing instead that the vessels were creative works designed to explore abstract concepts—not literal records of identifiable people's actual features.

steps.title: Apply to the Text 1 interpretation
steps.content: Trever contends that these facial markings are artistic devices—not literal records—through which sculptors explored abstract ideas. Choices A, B, and C either make irrelevant comparisons (A), reinforce the “hyper-realistic” reading Trever opposes (B), or raise points outside the texts (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
